questions.text: What was the season that everyone was talking about?
questions.explanation: It was the season for Hilsa fish, and fishermen could think of nothing else.

questions.text: The king was tired of hearing about ________ from everyone.
questions.explanation: Even the courtiers were talking about Hilsa fish all the time.

questions.text: The king challenged ________ to buy a huge Hilsa and bring it to the palace without anyone asking about it.
questions.explanation: Gopal was a clever courtier known for his wit.

questions.text: What did Gopal do to his face before going to buy the fish?
questions.explanation: He half-shaved his face as part of his disguise to look like a madman.

questions.text: Gopal smeared ________ on his body before leaving.
questions.explanation: He rubbed ash on himself to further enhance his ridiculous appearance.

questions.text: What was Gopal's wife's reaction to his appearance?
options: She thought he was going mad
questions.explanation: She was shocked and asked him why he was dressing in such a disgraceful fashion.

questions.text: When Gopal went to the market, people thought he was a:
questions.explanation: His half-shaven face, ash-smeared body, and rags made him look like a lunatic.

questions.text: Did anyone in the market ask Gopal about the Hilsa fish he was carrying?
questions.explanation: People were so distracted by his appearance that they forgot to notice the fish.

questions.text: What happened when Gopal reached the palace gates?
options: The guards refused to let him in
questions.explanation: The guards didn't recognize him and wouldn't let a 'madman' inside.

questions.text: How did Gopal get the king's attention at the palace?
options: He started to dance and sing loudly
questions.explanation: His loud singing and dancing forced the king to order the guards to bring him in.

questions.text: What was the king's reaction when he realized the 'madman' was Gopal?
options: He was surprised and asked why he was dressed like that
questions.explanation: The king recognized Gopal but was confused by his attire.

questions.text: Gopal reminded the king about ________ when he stood before him with the fish.
questions.explanation: Gopal showed that he had successfully completed the impossible challenge.

questions.text: The king ________ at the end when he realized Gopal had won.
questions.explanation: The king congratulated Gopal on achieving the impossible once again.

questions.text: The story of Gopal and the Hilsa Fish is presented in the form of a:
questions.explanation: It uses illustrations and speech bubbles to tell the witty tale.

questions.text: What does the story tell us about Gopal?
options: He was very clever and witty
questions.explanation: Gopal used his intelligence to outsmart the obvious obsession of the people.

questions.text: Fishermen sold Hilsa fish to ________ in the palace.
questions.explanation: Even the high-ranking courtiers were interested in the prices of Hilsa.

questions.text: The word 'disgraceful' used by Gopal's wife means:
questions.explanation: She was embarrassed by how he looked in rags and ash.

questions.text: Gopal's strategy was to create a bigger ________ than the fish itself.
questions.explanation: By looking ridiculous, he made himself the center of attention.

questions.text: The 'Hilsa' is a popular fish found in:
questions.explanation: It is a well-known fish in the Indian subcontinent, especially in Bengal.

questions.text: The King lost his ________ when a courtier asked him about Hilsa fish.
questions.explanation: He was so frustrated with the constant fish talk that he snapped at the courtier.

questions.text: Gopal put on ________ clothes to look like a beggar.
questions.explanation: Rags helped him look the part of a crazy man.

questions.text: Why did Gopal half-shave his face?
questions.explanation: It was a deliberate part of his 'madman' costume.

questions.text: The courtier who was scolded by the king felt:
questions.explanation: He dropped his eyes in shame after the king's outburst.

questions.text: What was the 'impossible' task according to the king?
options: Stopping people from talking about Hilsa
questions.explanation: The challenge was to bring the fish without anyone mentioning it.

questions.text: Gopal walked a long distance to the palace with the ________.
questions.explanation: He carried the very thing people were obsessed with, yet no one noticed it.

questions.text: Gopal's wife thought he was 'smearing' ash. Smearing means:
questions.explanation: He was rubbing the ash onto his skin.

questions.text: The people in the palace thought Gopal was a 'comical' character. Comical means:
questions.explanation: His appearance was so absurd that it provoked laughter and curiosity.

questions.text: The story teaches us that ________ can solve any problem.
questions.explanation: Gopal's clever thinking turned the tables on the king's challenge.

questions.text: Who said, 'I accept the challenge, Your Majesty'?
questions.explanation: Gopal confidently took up the king's bet.

questions.text: Which of these words describes the king's state of mind at the start of the story?
questions.explanation: He was fed up with the non-stop talk about Hilsa fish.

questions.text: The guards told Gopal, 'You can't see the ________.'
questions.explanation: They barred his entry because he looked like a dangerous or crazy beggar.

questions.text: What was the main focus of the people in the market?
questions.explanation: They were entirely occupied with Gopal's bizarre appearance.

questions.text: How many people asked Gopal about the Hilsa fish on his journey?
questions.explanation: Not a single person mentioned the fish until he reached the king.

questions.text: The king ________ Gopal when he proved his point.
questions.explanation: He was impressed by Gopal's success.

questions.text: Gopal's wife tried to ________ him from going out.
questions.explanation: She was worried about his sanity and social reputation.

questions.text: Gopal said, 'I am going to buy a ________.'
questions.explanation: He stated his goal clearly to his wife, even if she didn't believe his methods.

questions.text: Which part of Gopal's plan was most effective?
questions.explanation: The total effect of his appearance was what created the perfect distraction.

questions.text: The king's palace was ________ by a large crowd of people.
questions.explanation: A crowd had gathered to watch the 'madman' singing outside.

questions.text: Was the king's challenge fair?
options: Yes, it was a test of Gopal's famous wit
questions.explanation: The king knew Gopal was the only one who could possibly do it.

questions.text: Gopal's achievement was called ________ by the king.
questions.explanation: Bringing the fish unnoticed during the Hilsa season was considered an impossible feat.
